items.front: 6-7-1: How to determine if ceiling meets circling minima for timed approaches?
items.back: Compare MDA (MSL) with ceiling (AGL) PLUS the airport elevation.

items.front: 6-7-2a: Three elements of a timed approach clearance for the succeeding aircraft
items.back: 1. Clear for approach. 2. Descend to altitude vacated by preceding aircraft. 3. Leave FAF/OM/fix-in-lieu inbound at specified time. (Or vector to cross fix per 6-7-5 when using radar.)
2 minutes or 5-mile radar interval between successive approaches.

items.front: 6-7-5b: En route wake turbulence timed approach intervals — behind super
items.back: Heavy → 3 min / 6 mi. Large → 3 min / 7 mi. Small → 4 min / 8 mi.

items.front: 6-7-5c: Terminal wake turbulence timed approach intervals — behind Category A
items.back: Cat B/C/D → 3 min / 6 mi. Cat E/F/G → 3 min / 7 mi. Cat H/I → 4 min / 8 mi.

items.front: 6-7-5d: Four factors for increasing the timed approach interval
items.back: 1. Relative speeds. 2. Existing weather. 3. Distance between approach fix and airport. 4. Type of approach.

items.front: 6-7-7a: Precautionary action when weather suggests a missed approach
items.back: Issue an ALTERNATIVE missed approach procedure to the next aircraft.

items.front: 6-7-7c: When 6-7-2b applies and first aircraft goes missed — what happens to the second aircraft?
items.back: Retain radar control OR clear second aircraft to maintain last assigned altitude and RETURN TO HOLDING/APPROACH FIX to hold until traffic permits approach clearances.
